args.thought: Plan the investigation into Zscaler's stock selloff. I need to: 1) Get recent price action data to confirm the selloff magnitude and timing, 2) Search for recent news/earnings that triggered the decline, 3) Check analyst ratings changes or downgrades, 4) Look for sector-wide vs company-specific issues, 5) Examine any fundamental concerns (guidance, competition, margins). Start with price data and news to identify the catalyst.

Zacks Research Downgrades Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) to Strong Sell

Written by MarketBeat

February 28, 2026

Zscaler Inc. (NASDAQ: ZS) on Thursday reported better-than-expected second-quarter financial results and raised its FY26 sales guidance. Quarterly revenue came in at $815.75 million which beat the analyst consensus estimate of $798.82 million. results.title: Zscaler posts wider quarterly loss on higher spending; shares down 9% | Reuters
results.author: Jaspreet Singh
highlights:
Zscaler's second-quarter revenue up 26% year-on-year
- Company's expenses rise sharply on sales and R&D costs - Zscaler forecast third-quarter revenue above estimates Feb 26 (Reuters) - Zscaler (ZS.O), opens new tab on Thursday posted a wider net loss in the second quarter, citing higher spending on sales, marketing, and research and development in a competitive market, sending shares of the cloud security firm down roughly 9% in extended trading. The results come when IT budgets remain tight and clients spend cautiously on large deals amid economic uncertainty, even though cybersecurity budgets face less pressure than general capital outlays.  ...    Zscaler provides cloud-based zero trust security, which is designed to eliminate the need for legacy firewalls and virtual private networks (VPNs) by authenticating each connection rather than granting broader network access. Shares of cybersecurity companies, including CrowdStrike (CRWD.O), opens new tab and Zscaler, have fallen in recent days as investors assessed the potential impact of AI startup Anthropic's Claude Code Security  ...    Zscaler, which competes with Palo Alto Networks (PANW.O), opens new tab and Cloudflare (NET.N), opens new tab, reported a net loss of $34.3 million in the quarter ended January 31, widening sharply from a net loss of $7.7 million a year earlier. Total operating expenses rose to $676.3 million in the second quarter, compared with $539.5 million in the year-ago  ...    "While CIOs (chief information officers) care about the budget, but they're doing two things: they do want to embrace  ...    Businesses spend on tools offered by companies such as Zscaler amid a wave of high-profile cyberattacks that has hit companies, including F5 (FFIV.O), opens new tab. Zscaler said its second-quarter revenue rose 26% to $815.8 million, beating analysts' average estimate of $798.8 million, according to LSEG-compiled data. Its adjusted profit per share of $1.01 exceeded estimate of 90 cents. The company forecast third-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.00 to $1.01, above analysts' estimates of 95 cents, and revenue of $834 million to $836 million, topping expectations of $831.9 million. Reporting by Jaspreet Singh in Bengaluru; Editing by Tasim Zahid and Sherry Jacob-Phillips Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles., opens new tab

results.title: Why is Zscaler Down? Evaluating the Q2 2026 Selloff and Key Support | Oversold
highlights:
# Why is Zscaler Down? Evaluating the Q2 2026 Selloff and Key Support Zscaler stock is down over 56% from its all-time high after a punishing Q2 2026 earnings reaction. We break down why ZS is dropping and key levels to watch. AuthorMichael Wolf

March 7, 2026

---

## The Crucial Question: Will the $135.80 Support Hold? For investors wondering why Zscaler is down so dramatically, the answer requires looking beyond the headline earnings numbers and evaluating the broader market mechanics at play. Currently trading at $164.06, the cloud security giant is sitting more than 56% below its all-time high of $376.11, a peak established over four years ago in November 2021. The recent price action has driven the stock dangerously close to a vital technical support zone at $135.80. When an asset sheds more than half of its peak value, market psychology inherently shifts from eagerly buying the dip to carefully determining where the actual structural floor might be. A healthy test of this $135.80 support level would  ...    that the institutional distribution phase has finally exhausted itself. Conversely, if ZS breaks  ...    To understand exactly why Zscaler is going down, we have to examine the immediate aftermath of its fiscal second-quarter 2026 earnings report from late February. On the surface, the headline numbers looked like a resounding success. Zscaler reported quarterly revenue of $815.8 million and adjusted earnings of $1.01 per share, both comfortably beating Wall Street consensus estimates. Yet, instead of rallying, the stock experienced a severe double-digit selloff, plunging to fresh local lows. This counterintuitive drop boils down to three core catalysts: - Macroeconomic Headwinds: The broader cybersecurity sector is currently navigating tighter enterprise IT budgets, prolonged elevated interest rates, and a market regime shift. However, the weekly RSI has plummeted to 29.8. It is  ...    In terms of price action, we saw a lower pivot established on February 27, 2026, at $140.56. This aggressive flush  ...    triple-digit forward price-to-earnings ratios. Investors were more than willing to pay an exorbitant premium for uninterrupted hyper-growth. Today, the fundamental picture looks vastly different. Following the company's raised fiscal 2026 earnings guidance of approximately $4.00 per share, ZS now trades at a forward P/E ratio of roughly 41x. While this still represents a notable premium to the broader S&P 500, it  ...    Despite the stock's poor performance, it is important to note that the underlying business is not fundamentally broken.  ...    A drop of this magnitude is naturally starting to draw attention from value-oriented tech investors who may have felt priced out of Zscaler during its previous multi-year run. args.thought: I have sufficient data to explain the Zscaler selloff. The key catalysts are clear: 1) Q2 earnings beat but wider GAAP loss ($34.3M vs $7.7M prior year), 2) Higher operating expenses ($676.3M vs $539.5M YoY) on sales/marketing and R&D, 3) Multiple analyst price target cuts despite maintaining buy ratings, 4) Zacks downgraded to Strong Sell, 5) Macro headwinds in cybersecurity sector with tight IT budgets. The stock fell from ~52-week high of $336.99 to current ~$140-150 range (down ~56% from ATH). Current price around $140-164 depending on source. Analyst mean target $233.70 suggests upside but targets were recently slashed.
